summary: The predominant type of hemoglobin found in humans, especially among adults without specific genetic disorders such as sickle cell anemia, is HbA at approximately 95%-98%. This high prevalence underscores its stability across various conditions making it a reliable marker for general health assessments rather than pathological states where alternative forms might be more significant but less common under normal circumstances. To directly answer your query regarding "in human", we can confidently state that most individuals have predominantly HbA within their red blood cells contributing significantly over time due to natural selection pressures promoting efficient oxygen transport mechanisms supported primarily through this form of globulin molecule structure. Agent1 concludes based largely upon widespread presence and consistency observed globally amongst different ethnicities when no underlying disease processes are present influencing otherwise standard
